column1: csobod: If it wasn't for the damn komi ...
csobod: Oh, only .5 komi. Well, then I just messed up.
TimH: I was lucky you didn't take the group at F4 early in the game
csobod: I can't believe I messed that up. I was really kicking myself for that one.
csobod: Once I realized my blunder with the lower right group, I knew it was going to be a short fight.
TimH: You just about gave me a heart attack when you captured the group around Q5
csobod: Hahahaha, yeah but then I filled in an eye.
C[hilger: great game!  never had such wide open space like that
TimH: Agreed. It was mostly a very peaceful game
C[TimH: Yo, how goes it?  Today was day 4 at my new job in San Jose.
csobod: It goes, it goes. How are you liking it thus far?
TimH: So far, so good.  The people are interesting, a couple dogs wander the office during the day and a bunch of people actually showed up in costume today.
C[TimH: So far, so good.  The people are interesting, a couple dogs wander the office during the day and a bunch of people actually showed up in costume today.
C[csobod: Hah, excellent. Stephanie and I recently went to i3 (the makerspace in Ferndale) for their Halloween party. We went dressed as ""hipster Game of Thrones.""
C[TimH: Sounds good...Lannisters, Starks or Baratheon?
C[csobod: None of the above. She went as Hipster Melissandre and I went as Hipster Dondarrion.
C[TimH: the night is dark and full of terrors...
C[TimH: Have you been playing much?  This is my first game since the last Go club I went to in MI
C[csobod: Not as much as I should. Some teaching games, and of course getting schooled by Nithin.
C[csobod: Hmm. I'm going to have to think on this one. I'm going to head to bed. Talk to you tomorrow!
